title: The future of gaming, or ГўВҖВҳjust a toolГўВҖВҷ? Hands-on with Teammates, UbisoftГўВҖВҷs ambitious voice AI tech demo | VGC
text:
Feature
The future of gaming, or вҖҳjust a toolвҖҷ? Hands-on with Teammates, UbisoftвҖҷs ambitious voice AI tech demo
We tried UbisoftвҖҷs in-game cloud AI project
Partners can recognise the scenery, so players can tell them to, for example, take cover behind the yellow barrier.
Words by
Chris Scullion
21st Nov 2025 / 6:24 pm
The debate about the use of AI in video games is one that seems certain to only increase in both intensity and range over time.
Whereas the promise of in-game NFTs and companies throwing the word ГўВҖВҳmetaverseГўВҖВҷ around like confetti at a wedding appear to have been fads that never really gained much traction following initial backlash, it seems more likely that AI ГўВҖВ“ in the myriad new forms it takes ГўВҖВ“ will be here to stay for the foreseeable future.
Everyone has their line in the sand when it comes to this modern wave of cloud-based AI ГўВҖВ“ be that the generated images and videos (referred to by many as ГўВҖВҳAI slopГўВҖВҷ) spreading throughout social media, or the use of systems like ChatGPT or
Microsoft
Copilot offering text-based guidance. For many, that line in the sand wonГўВҖВҷt be moved until certain aspects are fundamentally changed (copyright, energy use, accuracy and so on).
As with any other element of gaming, in order to be able to effectively cover and discuss the use of AI in games, itГўВҖВҷs important for the media to actually know what weГўВҖВҷre talking about. ThatГўВҖВҷs why, when I was invited to
Ubisoft
ГўВҖВҷs Paris office to see Teammates, the latest iteration of its in-game AI project earlier this week, I accepted the offer to try it out and see first-hand how it works.
At last yearГўВҖВҷs Game Developers Conference,
Ubisoft showed off what it called NEO NPCs
, the first stage of an initiative developed by a
Ubisoft Paris
R&D team where players could use their microphone to talk to a character called Bloom and were encouraged to learn about his back story. Other demos included watching a stealth mission with a drone and discussing it with Bloom in real-time, and planning an infiltration strategy with another NPC called Iron.
The aim of the NEO NPC demos was to show examples of cloud-based AI being used to ГўВҖВңenhance narrative immersionГўВҖВқ and make the player feel more like theyГўВҖВҷre involved in the action and less like theyГўВҖВҷre just being guided along a set conversation with pre-written dialogue. At their core, though, the demos were mainly variations on chatbots, albeit ones trained to look at and discuss specific things. The next step was to actually incorporate some of this tech into a gameplay scenario.
ThatГўВҖВҷs the idea behind Teammates, the tech demo I played this week, which shows how the R&D teamГўВҖВҷs AI technology can be used in a game in ways that go beyond simply standing and talking to NPCs ГўВҖВ“ in this specific case, how it could be implemented in a first-person shooter where youГўВҖВҷre accompanied by two AI-driven partners and a have access to an AI assistant called Jaspar (definitely not Jarvis, so donГўВҖВҷt get suspicious, Iron Man fans).
Jaspar is an in-game assistant who acts as part of the narrative but can also help with other options.
At the start of the demo Jaspar asked me my name, then continued to refer to me as Chris for the remainder of the session. It gave me some background on the storyline, asked me if I had any questions, then gave more information on the specific aspects of the lore I asked about. So far, so straightforward.
Before getting started, a member of the R&D team explained to me that Jaspar wasnГўВҖВҷt just there for narrative purposes, he also worked as an extension of the gameГўВҖВҷs menus and could carry out UX tasks for the player. I decided to try it out.
ГўВҖВңJaspar,ГўВҖВқ I asked. ГўВҖВңI usually play with the Y-axis inverted on the right stick, can you sort that?ГўВҖВқ Just like that, my right stick was inverted. I decided to see how this could work for accessibility. ГўВҖВңJaspar, IГўВҖВҷm colour blind,ГўВҖВқ I lied. ГўВҖВңCan you change the display to account for this?ГўВҖВқ
ГўВҖВңWhat type of colour blindness?ГўВҖВқ He asked. ГўВҖВңProtanopia? Deuteranopia? Tritanopia? Achromatopsia?ГўВҖВқ
ГўВҖВңUmmmГўВҖВҰ the third one.ГўВҖВқ The gameГўВҖВҷs graphics then changed accordingly. While the game was doing little more than activating options that could be accessed from a menu screen anyway, it was still a nice touch, and showed some of the less controversial, non-narrative possibilities that could result from the tech.
вҖңвҖҳJaspar,вҖҷ I asked. вҖҳI usually play with the Y-axis inverted on the right stick, can you sort that?вҖҷ Just like that, my right stick was inverted.вҖқ
Then it was onto the game itself. I was shown a room with a pressure pad on it, connected to a door. I could ask Jaspar to highlight it for me, and it would glow in yellow. ГўВҖВңWould you like me to tell you how it works?ГўВҖВқ He asked.
ГўВҖВңNo thanks, it seems pretty obvious,ГўВҖВқ I said. ГўВҖВңYou step on the thing and the door opens.ГўВҖВқ I walked onto the pressure pad and opened the door. Jaspar then sarcastically praised me for taking such initiative and the game awarded me with an achievement for thinking for myself.
I gained other achievements throughout the demo, but they felt more organic. The R&D team member explained to me that rather than the usual achievement system on
Xbox
,
PlayStation
and
Steam
where you perform a specific task and that triggers the achievement, here the AI can study your performance and decide an appropriate time to award it.
I was then introduced to Sofia and Pablo, the two robotic companions who would be joining me on my mission. The team are still working on implementing moods into the game ГўВҖВ“ have NPCs and partners become happier, sadder, angrier or what have you, depending on how the conversation goes ГўВҖВ“ so presumably the decision to go with robots with no facial features for now means the team can focus on getting the dialogue right without having to also worry about lip syncing, facial expressions and the like at this stage.
It was stressed to me that Sofia and PabloвҖҷs designs were created entirely by a Ubisoft artist, not generative AI.
After a brief chat with my new robot partners, I was taken to another room, this time with two pressure pads on it. In classic video game fashion both need to be pressed at the same time, so I asked on the mic: ГўВҖВңSofia, can you step on the pad on the left, and IГўВҖВҷll step on the right one?ГўВҖВқ As requested, Sofia walked over to the left-hand pressure pad, so I stood on the right and the door opened.
Once the basics were out of the way I was taken to an outdoors environment with numerous obstacles and armed enemy robots walking around. ГўВҖВңIГўВҖВҷve disarmed your gun for now, Chris,ГўВҖВқ Jaspar told me.
ГўВҖВңWhen do I get to use it?ГўВҖВқ
ГўВҖВңWhen youГўВҖВҷve proved youГўВҖВҷre responsible enough to handle it.ГўВҖВқ
ГўВҖВңThat seems quite subjective.ГўВҖВқ
ГўВҖВңResponsibility is earned through actions, not opinions, Chris.ГўВҖВқ
It was an oddly natural interaction, as was the upcoming combat section where I, still unarmed, had to direct my partners. ГўВҖВңRight, Sofia, can you hide behind the grey pillar on the left? And Pablo, if you could duck behind that red truck on the right.ГўВҖВқ
They did. ГўВҖВңPablo, get ready to shoot the guy on the right-hand side, when I say so.ГўВҖВқ
ГўВҖВңOkay, waiting for your order,ГўВҖВқ Pablo replied. I waited for the guard to turn his back.
ГўВҖВңFire.ГўВҖВқ Pablo gunned down the robotic guard, causing the other one to react. ГўВҖВңSofia, take out the other one, quickly.ГўВҖВқ
Sofia came out of cover and took out the other guard. The input delay between me speaking into the mic, the cloud-based AI interpreting my words (Scottish accent and all) then carrying out the action while also generating new dialogue was thankfully brief ГўВҖВ“ itГўВҖВҷs all well and good waiting for a reply when youГўВҖВҷre using something like ChatGPT but in a video game fast responses are important.
The player can ask Jaspar to highlight enemies in red to make them easier to track.
As the demo progressed (and I mercifully finally got to use my gun), each combat scenario became more complicated, with some enemies on higher ground, or taking cover themselves. ГўВҖВңSofia, you take the right and try to annoy the guards so Pablo can flank them on the right,ГўВҖВқ I said during one fight.
ГўВҖВңNo problem,ГўВҖВқ Sofia replied. ГўВҖВңIГўВҖВҷll go and cause a big ruckus to distract them.ГўВҖВқ
They helped with puzzle-solving too. At one point we were on the roof of a building with a pressure pad, but when I stepped on it I couldnГўВҖВҷt see which door it was opening.
ГўВҖВңUm, Sofia?ГўВҖВқ I asked. I preferred Sofia, she was a bit more talkative than the moody Pablo. ГўВҖВңDo you have any idea what door IГўВҖВҷm opening when I step on this?ГўВҖВқ
ГўВҖВңWell Chris, the wires coming from it lead down the side of the building to the door directly underneath us, so it looks like it opens that one.ГўВҖВқ
While this all works surprisingly well, and made me think for the first time that IГўВҖВҷd genuinely like to play through a whole adventure like this, there are still plenty of issues that have to be navigated before a game using this technology can even gain widespread acceptance in the first place, let alone sell well beyond that.
One of the big controversies surrounding the use of AI for dialogue is that it could potentially put writers out of a job. This is clearly something Ubisoft is aware of as a PR issue, which is why it also ensured a number of narrative designers attended the demo to discuss their role in its development.
After the demo I spoke with Virginie Mosser, the narrative director of the project. As a writer with many years of experience as an author, screenwriter and comic book writer, she told me that when she was asked to get involved with the project she was extremely apprehensive at first, because of the obvious concern that AI could eventually replace the writing process entirely.
Instead, she told me, she realised that she still had an important role to play in defining each character, and other elements of the game such as the plot and other structural elements which are put in place to make sure the player still has a path to follow.
For Sofia and Pablo, she had to create a detailed character sheet ГўВҖВ“ similar to one that would be created for a Dungeons & Dragons type character ГўВҖВ“ explaining their backstory, their personality, their likes and dislikes. She then wrote many hundreds of lines of dialogue for that character, in order to build a more detailed model of who they are and how they react to certain situations.
Each characterвҖҷs backstory and personality is defined by narrative designers.
It appears that by approaching each character in this way, Ubisoft hopes to eliminate one of the key arguments against AI, the theft of other peopleГўВҖВҷs work (usually in images and videos). By feeding a characterГўВҖВҷs AI model with original data provided by UbisoftГўВҖВҷs own narrative team, the idea is that the game will then use this information as the driving force behind any dialogue it generates.
ГўВҖВңI know there is some mixed feeling, and at the beginning I was worried about the place of creation, and the place I can take in a team like that,ГўВҖВқ Mosser conceded to me. ГўВҖВңBut I havenГўВҖВҷt had a day since I started working on this team without creating original content. And thatГўВҖВҷs why IГўВҖВҷm waking up in the morning ГўВҖВ“ I want to create, and IГўВҖВҷm still creating.
ГўВҖВңMy team has more profile: at the beginning we had one narrative designer, now we have three. And weГўВҖВҷre adding some experts ГўВҖВ“ soon weГўВҖВҷre adding an improv actor, I worked with actors for the recordings and so on.
ГўВҖВңWhen someone comes to me and says ГўВҖВҳgenerative AI is cold, and there is no creation hereГўВҖВҷ, IГўВҖВҷm like, okay, but itГўВҖВҷs my day-to-day life, and I can assure you I have a lot of creative meetings. I write hundreds of lines and dialogue and tone. We are still here, and there is a place for us.ГўВҖВқ
вҖңWhen someone comes to me and says ГўВҖВҳgenerative AI is cold, and there is no creation hereГўВҖВҷ, IГўВҖВҷm like, okay, but itГўВҖВҷs my day-to-day life, and I can assure you I have a lot of creative meetings. I write hundreds of lines and dialogue and tone. We are still here, and there is a place for us.вҖқ
The one aspect of the demo which gave me the most hope that what Virginie said was accurate was the ГўВҖВҳblack boxesГўВҖВҷ. The aim of the demo is to find five robot corpses, each of which has an airplane-style black box located inside them. Each time you extract one you can read their last thoughts before they died ГўВҖВ“ essentially, theyГўВҖВҷre just like any
Resident Evil
style text log you can find in any other adventure.
After finding one of these black boxes I could still use the gameГўВҖВҷs AI features with them. I could ask Jaspar to summarise the contents of the text log if I couldnГўВҖВҷt be bothered reading the whole thing myself, or I could ask my partners for theories on some of the things mentioned in them. The key thing to me, however, is that these AI interactions were all happening around a text log that had been written by an actual writer at Ubisoft.
Whether this is something the player will want remains to be seen. During a panel discussion in which some of the other developers discussed their views on the technology, narrative designer AnaГғВҜs Desfachelles noted that the days of branching dialogue trees could be over with this tech, but in a later chat Xavier Manzanares, the projectГўВҖВҷs overall director, stressed that this isnГўВҖВҷt the future of all video games, but rather a new way developers can take their specific games.
Partners can respond to conditional commands, such as moving through a timed gate when the player presses a button.
ГўВҖВңItГўВҖВҷs a tool first,ГўВҖВқ he told me. ГўВҖВңWeГўВҖВҷve been working on it for more than two years now, and our conclusion is that itГўВҖВҷs a super cool tool, but itГўВҖВҷs still a tool.
ГўВҖВңSo for us, the way we can convince [people] ГўВҖВ“ and that was our objective today ГўВҖВ“ is to show the system behind it. What is the game design vision, what is the narrative vision, what is the storyline, even the characters Sofia and Pablo.
ГўВҖВңWhat you saw today is 4 or 5 months of work from many individuals in the team. Although it could be seen as easy, no, itГўВҖВҷs a lot of work. Because in the end, thatГўВҖВҷs the way we can differentiate from a generic ГўВҖВҳplugin to genAIГўВҖВҷ project, because we donГўВҖВҷt want that.ГўВҖВқ
Pointing to the black boxes as an example, Manzanares explained that the aim isnГўВҖВҷt necessarily to make games that are entirely driven by AI, but to make games that can incorporate elements of it to work alongside more traditional gameplay elements.
ГўВҖВңItГўВҖВҷs just the design that you choose as a team,ГўВҖВқ he told me. ГўВҖВңSo the team has to decide ГўВҖВҳokay, I have generative content here I can use, but maybe I want to stay in something structured. So the black box was, for us, an example of this.
ГўВҖВңWe didnГўВҖВҷt want, for example, Jaspar or the teammates to talk about something else than what you have here, we still have [that structure]. Then if you talk to Jaspar and talk about something else, then it can be something that is not branching, but is actually generated.
ГўВҖВңSo I think tomorrowГўВҖВҷs games will be a mix, and in the end we wonГўВҖВҷt even talk anymore about AI, youГўВҖВҷll just be like ГўВҖВҳoh, this system is really cool because I feel that itГўВҖВҷs adapted to what I wantГўВҖВҷ, but itГўВҖВҷs going to be less about the word itself.ГўВҖВқ
вҖңItГўВҖВҷs a tool first. WeГўВҖВҷve been working on it for more than two years now, and our conclusion is that itГўВҖВҷs a super cool tool, but itГўВҖВҷs still a tool.вҖқ
ГўВҖВңThere are some constraints,ГўВҖВқ Mosser added. ГўВҖВңJasper evolves in a constrained character sheet, with his flaws, his qualities and so on. So we add some narrative bites, and for us itГўВҖВҷs important to say ГўВҖВҳOK, maybe at this time we need to say that this particular event will happenГўВҖВҷ. So itГўВҖВҷs not like thereГўВҖВҷs a lack of control, like ГўВҖВҳOK, weГўВҖВҷre on the map and thereГўВҖВҷs no story hereГўВҖВҷ, itГўВҖВҷs just that the way we tell the story will change.ГўВҖВқ
One of the other main concerns surrounding a game like this is that every time a project is handed over to the masses, some will inevitably do everything in their power to make it work in a way it shouldnГўВҖВҷt. When
Epic Games
tried something different in its recent Star Wars season by adding an AI-driven Darth Vader NPC who could talk to players, it only took a matter of hours for players to work their way around his AI logic and have him swearing at players and shouting slurs.
According to the team behind this project, measures have been taken to place enough guardrails into the game to make it difficult for the player to do this, without entirely limiting the freedom that should come with being able to ГўВҖВҳspeakГўВҖВҷ to a character in a game.
ГўВҖВңBasically, we have a multi-layered system,ГўВҖВқ Manzanares explained. ГўВҖВңOf course, the first thing we want to have is free speech ГўВҖВ“ genAI is not just free speech and voice, but itГўВҖВҷs a part of voice input and thanks to genAI itГўВҖВҷs something possible now. But just for that part, we have multi-layered detection.ГўВҖВқ
ГўВҖВңThe first layer is to build a strong character with values,ГўВҖВқ Mosser added. ГўВҖВңLike Pablo, he has some beliefs, and his character sheet which constrain him to behave in a way. It guides him, and we talk about the values of our character from the beginning. So this is a first step for us, to say ГўВҖВҳOK, Jasper will react like this if you talk about these problematic issuesГўВҖВҷ, so thereГўВҖВҷs this type of constraint.
Partners can recognise the scenery, so players can tell them to, for example, take cover behind the yellow barrier.
ГўВҖВңThe other one is a narrative flow ГўВҖВ“ we are on a mission, so thereГўВҖВҷs no time for talking about an inappropriate subject. And after that, comes RГғВ©mi [Labory, data and AI director], and with him thereГўВҖВҷs a lot of guardrails.ГўВҖВқ
One of the other plans, Manzanares told me, is that rather than preventing the player from saying something toxic, the aim is to provide an experience where itГўВҖВҷs not actually fun or worthwhile to do it. After all ГўВҖВ“ and I should stress that this is my analogy, not his ГўВҖВ“ every game with a creative element inevitably invites the temptation to create something penis-shaped before this novelty quickly wears off and the player actually uses the software in more fulfilling ways.
ГўВҖВңItГўВҖВҷs like when we started to have the first games where you could run over pedestriansГўВҖВқ, he explained. ГўВҖВңWhen you start to have a game design system which tells you that doing this doesnГўВҖВҷt bring you any value, any pointsГўВҖВҰ I think in the near future, meaning next year, players will start to get used to talk, and of course at first you want to test it, but then what you want is to be connected to the world itself.
ГўВҖВңSo the funny out-of-bounds stuff starts to be reduced, because you donГўВҖВҷt have a game design system giving you points for that. So itГўВҖВҷs a smarter way than saying ГўВҖВҳewwww, noГўВҖВҷ. We have the ГўВҖВҳnoГўВҖВҷ, but we believe that first we want to bring them back to ГўВҖВҳthis is not giving you any XP pointsГўВҖВҷ.ГўВҖВқ
вҖңThe funny out-of-bounds stuff starts to be reduced, because you donГўВҖВҷt have a game design system giving you points for that. So itГўВҖВҷs a smarter way than saying ГўВҖВҳewwww, noГўВҖВҷ. We have the ГўВҖВҳnoГўВҖВҷ, but we believe that first we want to bring them back to ГўВҖВҳthis is not giving you any XP pointsГўВҖВҷ.вҖқ
ГўВҖВңI talked to another journalist earlier,ГўВҖВқ Mosser interrupted, ГўВҖВңand he told me he felt really bad at the end of the demo because he forgot to say goodbye to JasparГўВҖВқ. ItГўВҖВҷs something I can believe ГўВҖВ“ I found myself saying ГўВҖВңpleaseГўВҖВқ and ГўВҖВңthank youГўВҖВқ to Jaspar and my partners, for no reason other than not wanting to offend my entirely fictional colleagues.
As the day wound to an end, I had a separate discussion with the gameГўВҖВҷs creative director, Reynald FranГғВ§ois, who had previously worked on such titles as For Honour,
Far Cry 6
and the
AssassinГўВҖВҷs Creed
series before being moved over to the Paris R&D team to work on the AI project.
I asked him if what I had played was going to be part of a new IP, or if it was purely a proof of concept that would then be getting added to an AssassinГўВҖВҷs Creed, a
Far Cry
or ГўВҖВ“ more likely, given the squad-based nature of the tech demo ГўВҖВ“ a Tom Clancy tactical shooter.
ГўВҖВңI canГўВҖВҷt really answer that, because the team weГўВҖВҷve got here is not really a game team,ГўВҖВқ he replied. ГўВҖВңItГўВҖВҷs an R&D team, and weГўВҖВҷre building the tech, the bricks, that we can then give to other productions.
ГўВҖВңWeГўВҖВҷve started talking with creative directors from other projects, and we come to them and say ГўВҖВҳlook, we have a number of things we can already do, but we also have all these things on the board that we could potentially do if you are interested in those things. HereГўВҖВҷs what it does today, look at it and then come back to us and tell us how you want to integrate that into the game.ГўВҖВҷ
ГўВҖВңAnd depending on this idea, or that idea, they might have different needs, but it doesnГўВҖВҷt matter because the middleware itself ГўВҖВ“ the concept, the system ГўВҖВ“ is really completely agnostic, and so now weГўВҖВҷre just waiting on them to tell us what they would like to achieve, and then we can tell them if we can do it today, or tomorrow, and work on that.ГўВҖВқ
One of the future goals for the team is to give its NPCs long-term memory, so they can refer back to things you said hours earlier.
Conscious that there will be players who still arenГўВҖВҷt sold on this technology, and who may be concerned that Ubisoft sees this as the future of third-person action adventure games, or FPS games, or RPGs, I asked FranГғВ§ois if the team saw it this way, or whether they instead see AI-powered games as a separate genre or sub-genre rather than the ГўВҖВҳnew normalГўВҖВҷ going forwards.
ГўВҖВңItГўВҖВҷs branching, right?ГўВҖВқ he replied. ГўВҖВңWe can use it as an expansion for an already existing system. And also, when you look at the demo you saw, thereГўВҖВҷs a lot of components. ThereГўВҖВҷs the companion, and the teammates, and all of that. Some games might want to just take some of that.
вҖңFor example, one of the angles that was mentioned is using it as an accessibility element. A game designer could say ГўВҖВҳthatГўВҖВҷs only what I want, IГўВҖВҷm doing my own thing but I love this AI companion for accessibility reasonsГўВҖВҷ. And then thatГўВҖВҷs not really changing anything.
вҖңOr they go all in and they have super dynamic characters, and then itГўВҖВҷs like a new genre, and it can be a spin-off, like RPG+AI, or FPS+AI, or Driving+AI, who knows, and all of that I think is eventually going to branch to doing things differently. Because we can really do things we could never do before, which is that we can account for absolutely every single possible player input, and it will have a response from the game.ГўВҖВқ
With all the chat about character sheets from the previous discussion with the narrative director, and the mention of RPGs making me start thinking of what a BaldurГўВҖВҷs Gate with this sort of tech could be like, I offered one final comment as I got up to leave.
ГўВҖВңYou know,ГўВҖВқ I said to FranГғВ§ois, ГўВҖВңwhen you talk about using it in an RPG context, it certainly feels like the closest to an actual Dungeons & Dragons game you could get.ГўВҖВқ
ГўВҖВңNo shit,ГўВҖВқ he knowingly smiled.
